top_categories.workout_context: 10,500ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At mile 60 of 106, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Étape du Dales' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 106 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
99ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Étape du Dales. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 10,500ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Étape du Dales' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 106 miles.
At Étape du Dales, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
106 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 106 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Tempo is Étape du Dales' race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 106 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Étape du Dales’s 106 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Étape du Dales with 10,500ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Durability, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 106 miles of mixed terrain in Settle, North Yorkshire, England.
